title: Dataset of electrophysiological signals (EEG, ECG, EMG) during Music therapy with adult burn patients in the Intensive Care Unit.
description: This dataset comprises electrophysiological signals (EEG, ECG, EMG) recorded from 9 adult burn patients in an intensive care unit during Music-Assisted Relaxation therapy sessions. As part of a randomized clinical trial (NCT04571255), recordings were obtained during baseline, intervention, and post-intervention phases across two separate sessions per patient. The study investigates the physiological effects of music therapy on pain perception and anxiety-depression levels in critically ill burn patients, with concurrent behavioral assessments using standardized clinical scales.
methods_description: Electrophysiological recordings were conducted using Micromed LTM64 equipment (sampling frequency ≥256 Hz) with 8-channel EEG (FP1, FP2, T3, T4, C3, C4, O1, O2; reference Cz, ground at mastoids), bipolar ECG (lead II), and bipolar EMG (corrugator supercilii muscle). Each session included three phases: pre-intervention baseline (eyes closed or fixed), Music-Assisted Relaxation intervention, and post-intervention recovery. Pain was assessed using Visual Analog Scale (0-10), and anxiety-depression using the Colombian version of the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS).
